name: Westover School
about: Westover School is an independent college-preparatory boarding and day school for girls in grades 9-12, located in Middlebury, Connecticut. Founded in 1909 by Mary Robbins Hillard, it enrolls roughly 192 students, about two-thirds of whom board. The school emphasizes the development of character, independence, and a sense of responsibility in young women.
college_matriculation: Graduates attend colleges including Northeastern University, Skidmore College, Syracuse University, Loyola University Maryland, and Saint Anselm College; 100% college matriculation.
financial_aid_notes: About 45% of families receive need-based aid, with over $4.5 million awarded annually. Aid is need-based and assessed via the Clarity Application.

name: ʻIolani School
about: ʻIolani School is a private, coeducational Episcopal college-preparatory school in Honolulu, Hawaiʻi, serving students from kindergarten through grade 12. Founded in 1863, it is one of the largest independent schools in the United States with about 2,200 students. A residence hall opened in 2019 provides boarding for students in grades 9-12.
college_matriculation: Graduates attend a wide range of competitive U.S. colleges and universities; details published in the school's College Counseling Profile.
financial_aid_notes: About 17% of students receive financial aid, with an average grant near $40,000; merit scholarships are also offered.

name: Kamehameha Schools
about: Kamehameha Schools is a private school system in Hawaiʻi established under the 1884 will of Princess Bernice Pauahi Bishop to educate children of Hawaiian ancestry. Its flagship Kapālama campus in Honolulu occupies about 600 acres and serves roughly 3,200 students, including about 550 boarders from the Neighbor Islands. Beginning in 2026-27 the schools will no longer charge tuition at their K-12 campuses.
notable_alumni: Duke Kahanamoku, Daniel K. Akaka, Kelly Hu, Brook Mahealani Lee, Auliʻi Cravalho
financial_aid_notes: A preference is given to students of Hawaiian ancestry; the system is supported by a large endowment, and beginning 2026-27 tuition will be eliminated at K-12 campuses.

name: Illinois Mathematics and Science Academy
about: The Illinois Mathematics and Science Academy (IMSA) is a three-year residential public secondary school in Aurora, Illinois, founded in 1985 to serve academically talented students in grades 10-12. All students live on campus, and Illinois residents pay no tuition or room-and-board, only a graduated student fee. IMSA emphasizes rigorous college-preparatory work in mathematics and science.
college_matriculation: Graduates attend universities including University of Illinois Urbana-Champaign, Northwestern, University of Chicago, MIT, Stanford, Harvard, Yale, University of Michigan, Cornell, and Carnegie Mellon; 100% college matriculation.
notable_alumni: Steve Chen (YouTube co-founder), Yu Pan (PayPal), Sam Yagan (OkCupid/SparkNotes), Clara Shih (Hearsay Social), Sabrina Gonzalez Pasterski
financial_aid_notes: As a public institution there is no tuition for Illinois residents; an annual student fee on a graduated scale (roughly $1,800-$19,000 over three years) may be reduced or waived by family income. Out-of-state students pay tuition of about $53,790.

name: Lake Forest Academy
about: Lake Forest Academy is an independent, coeducational college-preparatory boarding and day school for grades 9-12, located on a 150-acre campus in Lake Forest, Illinois, about 30 miles north of Chicago. Founded in 1857, it enrolls roughly 452 students from many states and countries, with about half boarding. The school requires athletics participation of every student.
college_matriculation: Graduates attend colleges including Northwestern University, Boston University, Duke University, New York University, and University of Michigan; 100% college-bound.
financial_aid_notes: The school offers over $6.3 million in financial aid grants annually, primarily need-based; merit scholarships range from $10,000 to $15,000 and require no separate application.

name: St. Bede Academy
about: Saint Bede Academy is a Roman Catholic, Benedictine coeducational college-preparatory school for grades 9-12 in Peru, Illinois, affiliated with Saint Bede Abbey. Founded in 1890, it enrolls about 271 students, roughly 10% of whom board. It began as an all-boys boarding school and became coeducational in 1973.
accreditation: North Central Association (Cognia); Diocese of Peoria Office of Catholic Education
notable_alumni: Andrew J. Bacevich (historian), J.A. Happ (MLB pitcher), James Massey (information theorist)
financial_aid_notes: Need-based tuition assistance and scholarships are available to students.
